top_signals.title: Europe urged to turn methane abatement into a maritime competitive advantage
top_signals.summary: Europe can strengthen its maritime competitiveness, reduce costs for businesses and consumers, and reinforce its climate policy by accelerating action on methane emissions from shipping, according to a new report published today by the Methane Abatement in Maritime Innovation Initiative (MAMII). The report, ‘Turning methane abatement into a competitive advantage: A five-point action plan for ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Japan's JGC bets on carbon-feeding bacteria to create bioplastics
Rural Japan hopes to charm domestic travelers priced out of overseas trips
OpenClaw AI agent found falling for phishing attacks, spills user data
top_signals.summary: Phishing simulation on an OpenClaw email agent with various configuration profiles showed that it was susceptible to tactics commonly used to compromise human users. [...]

top_signals.title: Al-Designed CTV Saves 100,000 Liters of Fuel, 258 Tons of CO2 a Year
top_signals.summary: Compute Maritime, the deep tech company behind NeuralShipper, the world's first Al platform for ship design, has revealed the results of its UK Government-funded project, GenDSOM, to bring generative Al and additive manufacturing into ship design.Working…
